pairs.question: Who was elected NFL commissioner in January 1960, and how many ballots did it take to secure the position?
pairs.answer: Pete Rozelle, the general manager of the Los Angeles Rams, was elected NFL commissioner on January 26, 1960. He was chosen as a compromise candidate on the twenty-third ballot.

pairs.question: Which two NFL teams played in the 1960 NFL Championship Game, and what was the final score?
pairs.answer: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Green Bay Packers by a score of 17-13 in the 1960 NFL Championship Game, held at Franklin Field in Philadelphia.

pairs.question: What notable post-season defeat did Vince Lombardi face in 1960, and what was his team's performance in the following years?
pairs.answer: Vince Lombardi's Green Bay Packers lost the 1960 NFL Championship Game to the Philadelphia Eagles. Despite this defeat, his team went on to win five NFL championship games in seven years and also won the first two Super Bowls.

pairs.question: What was the Playoff Bowl, and which teams participated in its inaugural game?
pairs.answer: The Playoff Bowl was a third-place game introduced by the NFL in 1960, played between the runners-up of each division after the NFL Championship Game. The inaugural Playoff Bowl featured the Detroit Lions and the Cleveland Browns, with the Lions winning by a score of 17-16.

pairs.question: How did the Baltimore Colts perform in the 1960 NFL season, particularly in the second half of the season?
pairs.answer: The Baltimore Colts, who were the two-time defending league champions, led the Western Division after their bye in week 9 but lost their final four games to finish with a .500 record at fourth place in the division.

pairs.question: What was the impact of the launch of the American Football League (AFL) in 1960 on the NFL?
pairs.answer: The AFL was launched in 1960 as a competitor to the NFL. The two leagues coexisted throughout the 1960s, eventually agreed to a merger in 1966, and became a single combined league in 1970.

pairs.question: Which team relocated from Chicago to St. Louis in March 1960, and what was the name of their baseball counterpart?
pairs.answer: The Chicago Cardinals NFL team relocated to St. Louis in March 1960 and were renamed the St. Louis Cardinals. This name was shared with the National League baseball club based in the same city.
